summary: Tested data-consistency fix on /investments page. PRIMARY fix VERIFIED: Shares & ETFs sub-tab now shows Total Value $845,000 (VGH 4000@$105=$420k, BHP 4000@$46.25=$185k, CBA 2000@$120=$240k) — matches Overview asset allocation. Overview regression cards all correct ($11.672M / $2.062M / $9.61M / +5.9% / 15 holdings). HOWEVER, regression broken on Cash & TDs sub-tab and Super & Pension sub-tab — these pages read from a DIFFERENT (old hardcoded) data source and were NOT updated to match the aligned CLIENT_DATA/CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1 values.

integration_issues.issue: Shows Total Cash $250,000 (Savings $70k = ING $45k + Ubank $25k; TDs $180k = Judo $100k + Macquarie $50k + BOQ $30k). Expected per data alignment: Cash ~$180,000 (Emergency Fund - ING Savings) + TD $350,000 (Westpac 12m) = $530,000. Page uses its own hardcoded dataset that does NOT read from CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1.investments.cash_savings / term_deposit_amount.
affected_selectors: Cash & TDs tab — Total Cash metric card

integration_issues.issue: Super Projection Calculator shows Primary (David) Super Balance 245000 and Spouse (Sarah) 198000 → Current Combined $443,000. Expected per CLIENT_DATA.thompson_family: David AustralianSuper $1,850,000 + Sarah REST Super $1,420,000 = $3,270,000. Also matches CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1.smsf_balance = 3270000 but calculator ignores that and uses old defaults (245000/198000 from CLIENT_FAMILY_DATA.client_1.familyMembers.superBalance).
affected_selectors: Super & Pension tab — Current Combined card

action_items: Fix Cash & TDs sub-tab to derive Total Cash and TD breakdown from CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1.investments.cash_savings ($180k) and term_deposit_amount ($350k), or split the $180k/$350k across the existing savings/TD line items so totals match the Overview donut/cards.

Fix Super & Pension sub-tab projection calculator to seed initial super balances from CLIENT_DATA.thompson_family super assets ($1.85M David + $1.42M Sarah = $3.27M combined) instead of the stale CLIENT_FAMILY_DATA.client_1 superBalance values (245000/198000). These are now the two remaining mis-aligned data sources.
After fixing, verify same consistency in adviser mode → /dashboard → UnifiedClientOverview → Investments sub-tab (not retested here since same components).
critical_code_review_comments: DRY / single-source-of-truth violation: CLIENT_FAMILY_DATA.client_1.familyMembers[].superBalance (245k/198k) and the hardcoded savings/TD list on the Cash & TDs page duplicate data already present in CLIENT_DATA.thompson_family.assets and CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1.investments. Bugs like this $845k vs $84.5k inconsistency will keep recurring until all investment views read from ONE seed (recommend CLIENT_DATA.thompson_family as the source of truth and derive CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA + CLIENT_FAMILY_DATA.superBalance from it).

SharePortfolio uses PortfolioProvider.sharePortfolio (DEFAULT_SHARE_PORTFOLIO) which has now been aligned ×10 quantities — good. But the other sub-tabs (Cash/TDs, Super/Pension, SMSF) each pull from different state slices; consider a single useClientPortfolio() hook returning normalized totals.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (3 of 6 acceptance criteria pass; Overview + Shares & ETFs + Overview regression pass; Cash & TDs + Super & Pension fail; adviser-mode mirror not independently retested)

test_credentials: None required — compliance modal bypassed via localStorage wealth_command_compliance_v5=permanent and app_mode=client
context_for_next_testing_agent: Shares $845k alignment is fully working. Two remaining mis-aligned data sources: (1) Cash & TDs page shows $250k total (should be $530k = $180k cash + $350k TD), and (2) Super & Pension calculator defaults to 245k+198k=443k (should be $3.27M from David+Sarah super in CLIENT_DATA). Both pages appear to use their own hardcoded arrays rather than CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1. After fix, retest both sub-tabs and also adviser-mode UnifiedClientOverview Investments sub-tab for the same 4 numbers.
rca of the issue: Only DEFAULT_SHARE_PORTFOLIO and CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1 top-level totals were updated in portfolioSeedData.js. The Cash & TDs page and Super & Pension calculator read from independent seed data (likely hardcoded arrays inside the sub-tab components, or CLIENT_FAMILY_DATA.client_1.familyMembers.superBalance for super). Fix must propagate aligned totals into those component-level data sources or refactor them to read from CLIENT_PORTFOLIO_DATA.client_1.investments.{cash_savings, term_deposit_amount, smsf_balance} (which are already correct at $180k / $350k / $3.27M).
